# KRChoksey — Usage Guide

*Mode: direct · 🔌 offline (no LLM) · requires: qpdf*

Part I of the KR Choksey → GnuCash flow. Simplifies the annual KR Choksey broker ledger-statement PDF into a clean, tied-out "Simplified Ledger" Excel workbook. Runs fully offline (deterministic, no LLM).

## When to use it

Run this first of the three KRChoksey sub-tabs. Its workbook is the input to "Reconcile" (Part II), which in turn feeds "GnuCash Import" (Part III).

## Inputs

- **KR Choksey Ledger Statement PDF** (required) — accepts: PDF (.pdf) — the ledger statement.
  - Your KR Choksey annual ledger statement PDF.
  - ⚠️ The PDF is password-protected; you must supply the password below.
- **PDF password (account holder's PAN)** (required) — accepts: Text — the account holder's PAN.
  - The PDF password — the account holder's PAN.
  - ⚠️ Decryption uses qpdf (bundled in the frozen build; on PATH for source mode).

## How to run

1. Select the KR Choksey ledger statement PDF.
2. Enter the PDF password (the account holder's PAN).
3. Click Run and download the Simplified Ledger workbook.

## Output

Output is written to the **`Data/outputs/`** folder, named:

```
Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-KRC-Ledger.xlsx
```

Files produced:

- **`YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-KRC-Ledger.xlsx`** — The Simplified Ledger: the annual statement re-laid as a clean, tied-out workbook. This is the file you pick in the Reconcile (Part II) tab.

## Tips

Run the three KRChoksey tabs in order — Ledger, then Reconcile, then GnuCash Import. Each later tab picks this output from a dropdown of prior runs.

## Troubleshooting

| If… | Then… |
|------|-------|
| Run fails opening the PDF. | Check the password is the correct PAN. Ensure qpdf is available in source mode. |
| Rows look incomplete. | Confirm you selected the full annual ledger statement, not a single contract note. |
